seoTitle: Create an Image Carousel in Webflow
metaDescription: How to build a responsive image carousel in Webflow using the native Slider element, without writing custom JavaScript.
intro: Displaying multiple product photos or portfolio pieces without slowing down your site or writing custom scripts is exactly what Webflow's native Slider component is for. This guide walks through dragging the slider onto your canvas, populating slides with images, and tuning navigation controls and autoplay behavior.

steps.heading: Add the Slider element
steps.body: Click the + Add Elements icon, scroll to the Advanced Components section, and drag the Slider element onto your page.

steps.heading: Set the slider height
steps.body: Select the slider in the Navigator, then assign it a fixed viewport height in the right sidebar style panel.

steps.heading: Add images to each slide
steps.body: Open each slide's Backgrounds menu and upload a high-resolution image, setting display to Cover and position to Center to avoid distortion.

steps.heading: Adjust cycling and loop settings
steps.body: Select the global slider wrapper and use the Gear icon to set auto-cycle timing and toggle infinite loop.

steps.heading: Style navigation and test responsiveness
steps.body: Customize the arrow colors and pagination dots, then use the responsive preview tabs to check swipe behavior across mobile breakpoints.

faq.question: How many slides can I add to the carousel?
faq.answer: You can keep adding slide containers as needed — the process of assigning a background image repeats for each one.

faq.question: How do I prevent my images from looking stretched?
faq.answer: Set the background image display mode to Cover and position to Center, which crops proportionally instead of distorting the image.

faq.question: Can I customize the arrow and dot navigation styling?
faq.answer: Yes, both the arrow elements and pagination dots are selectable in the Navigator and can be restyled with custom colors and sizes.

faq.question: Does the carousel work well on mobile?
faq.answer: Yes, swipe gestures are supported natively, and you should preview across breakpoints to confirm the layout scales properly.
